questions: how to contribute to open source
answers: Open source = software with publicly available source code. Anyone can view, modify, and distribute. Linux, Firefox, VS Code are examples.
Contribute: start with 'good first issue' labels, read contributing guide, fork repo, make changes, submit PR.
Benefits: free to use, community-driven improvement, transparency, no vendor lock-in, and great for learning.
How to start: find a project you use, read their docs, join their chat/forum, and pick a small issue to fix.
